steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: The sand at Kaihalulu Beach...is a strikingly different shade — a complete independent clause.
After the blank, the sentence has the structure though most sand gets its shade from..., deposits of iron oxide...lend the sand...a colorful red tint — a dependent clause + main clause that explains the shade.

A colon properly introduces the explanation that follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
Choice A: shade: though most — the colon introduces the explanation, and though most sand... begins a dependent clause that pairs with the main clause about iron oxide.

B is a comma splice (no terminal punctuation between full clauses). C wrongly punctuates though as a parenthetical. D wrongly ends the sentence with a dangling though.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
